subject: Physics
title: The Cosmic Web
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Cosmic Web.

hook.text: Galaxies aren't scattered randomly. They're arranged along vast filaments, forming a cosmic web stretching across the universe.

buildup.text: Dark matter's gravity shaped this structure. Galaxies formed along dense filaments with vast empty voids between them.

discovery.text: The largest structures in the cosmic web are superclusters—chains of galaxy clusters spanning hundreds of millions of light-years.

twist.text: The web looks remarkably like a neural network. The universe's structure echoes patterns in a human brain.

climax.text: Simulations that include dark matter reproduce the cosmic web perfectly. Without it, the structure vanishes.

punchline.text: A web of galaxies spanning the cosmos. Dark matter is the invisible scaffolding.

quiz.question: What shaped the large-scale structure of the cosmic web?
options: The expansion of light from the Big Bang
